marketState: Neutral, tilted bullish near-term on extreme cooling demand and crowded short positioning
summary: Henry Hub front-month sits at $2.90, essentially in line with the model's fair value of $2.923 and well inside its $2.423-$3.423 band, so there is no fair-value mispricing to trade off directly. The standout signal is weather: near-term demand is 7.9% above the 5-year norm and the 8-16 day outlook is running 43.3% above normal and above the historical max range, pointing to unusually strong cooling load that could slow storage injections over the next few weeks. That bullish demand impulse is reinforced by positioning - managed money is net short 105.7k contracts, in just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, a crowded short vulnerable to a squeeze on any bullish catalyst. Working against this: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise last week, daily/weekly technicals remain bearish (RSI high-30s, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA), and seasonality is weak with explicitly LOW reliability (59pp spread), so that edge should not be weighted heavily. Net effect: near-term upside risk from weather/positioning against a still-bearish structural backdrop argues for a cautious, moderate-conviction long bias rather than a high-conviction directional bet.

longTerm.sizing: 0.75R approx 0.75% account risk (trimmed for mixed storage/seasonality signals)
longTerm.rationale: Extreme 8-16D cooling demand (+43.3% vs 5y, above max range) should slow storage builds into peak summer, and managed money's crowded short (8th percentile 5y) leaves room for a squeeze; fair value itself rises seasonally to ~$3.03 by +21 days as the curve builds toward winter. Offsetting this, storage remains +180 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish surprise, and seasonality shows a slight downside tilt (38% odds higher in 3-4 weeks) albeit with explicitly low reliability.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D demand outlook reverts back toward/under the 5y norm, storage surprises continue bearish/larger-than-forecast, or price closes below the $2.70 stop confirming the daily/weekly downtrend is back in control.

shortTerm.rationale: 5m/15m momentum is bullish (RSI 61-63, MACD positive, 50>200 EMA) and near-term demand is already 7.9% above 5y norms, while price sits almost exactly at fair value ($2.90 vs $2.923) so there is no fair-value drag. However 1h is neutral and 4h/1d/1w remain bearish, and the front-to-next curve is in slight backwardation (-$0.032), so this is a tactical bounce/squeeze trade, not a trend-following one.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price fails to hold above ~$2.78 support, or 1h/4h technicals roll back to bearish, confirming the higher-timeframe downtrend is back in control.

keyDrivers: Extreme 8-16D cooling demand (+43.3% vs 5y, above max range) signals tighter injections ahead
Managed money net short at 8th percentile (5y) - crowded short, squeeze risk
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with bearish +22 Bcf surprise caps upside
Price sitting essentially at fair value ($2.90 vs $2.923 fair) - no mispricing edge
Daily/weekly technicals still bearish (RSI<40, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA)
risks: Seasonality reliability flagged LOW (59pp spread) - weak statistical edge this week
Storage builds could keep running above the 5y average, extending bearish pressure
Weather forecasts can shift quickly, reversing the demand-driven bullish case
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
